title: Is Mac Address update after restart pod container expected result?
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

<!--
Just wanted to confirm if Mac Address update in pod container expected result.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create pure IPv6 macvlan network.
2. Create container by specifying the above network with static ipv6address.
3. Check the Mac address.
4. Stop the container.
5. Start the container.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/100113781/182516476-c9392e57-26c8-4cb4-bd06-9ce266173279.png)

**Describe the results you received:**
The Mac address is updated after restart the container.

**Describe the results you expected:**
With the Mac address updated, the container cannot connect to other subnet. As a workaround, customer need to ping the gateway manually.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

comments.body: Please provide the full output of `podman info`, we need to know if you are on CNI or Netavark

For each podman start we configure a new network namespace and thus cretae a new interface every single time. If you want a static mac you can set one via cli (`--mac-address`).

Also please do not paste pictures of terminals, t is super hard to read this. Just copy and paste the text in a code block.

> For each podman start we configure a new network namespace and thus cretae a new interface every single time. If you want a static mac you can set one via cli (`--mac-address`).
> 
> Also please do not paste pictures of terminals, t is super hard to read this. Just copy and paste the text in a code block.

Thanks for your comments. You can close this one.

> Please provide the full output of `podman info`, we need to know if you are on CNI or Netavark

We are using CNI network
